steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The phrase "or SuperDARN, as space physicists like Nozomu Nishitani call it" is an appositive that provides an alternative name for the radar network. A dash already appears after "it" to close the appositive; the opening of the phrase must be set off by a matching dash after "Network" to form a proper paired-dash construction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Network,: Without an opening dash, the appositive is not properly framed, making the sentence grammatically ambiguous. ✗
  • Choice B — Network:: A colon after "Network" cannot pair with the closing dash and would incorrectly introduce the appositive. ✗
  • Choice C — Network: No punctuation after "Network" leaves the appositive without an opening mark to match the closing dash. ✗
  • Choice D — Network—: Places the required opening dash to match the closing dash after "it," correctly enclosing the appositive phrase. ✓
That is the correct answer.
